\u003cem\u003ecompany\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePaperclip is a Node.js server and React UI that orchestrates a team of AI agents to run a business. Bring your own agents, assign goals, and track your agents' work and costs from one dashboard.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt looks like a task manager — but under the hood it has org charts, budgets, governance, goal alignment, and agent coordination.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eManage business goals, not pull requests.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eStep\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eExample\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e01\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDefine the goal\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cem\u003e\"Build the #1 AI note-taking app to $1M MRR.\"\u003c/em\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e02\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eHire the team\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCEO, CTO, engineers, designers, marketers — any bot, any provider.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e03\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eApprove and run\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eReview strategy. Set budgets. Hit go. Monitor from the dashboard.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCOMING SOON: Clipmart\u003c/strong\u003e — Download and run entire companies with one click. Paperclip\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eWith Paperclip\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e❌ You have 20 Claude Code tabs open and can't track which one does what. On reboot you lose everything.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e✅ Tasks are ticket-based, conversations are threaded, sessions persist across reboots.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e❌ You manually gather context from several places to remind your bot what you're actually doing.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e✅ Context flows from the task up through the project and company goals — your agent always knows what to do and why.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e❌ Folders of agent configs are disorganized and you're re-inventing task management, communication, and coordination between agents.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e✅ Paperclip gives you org charts, ticketing, delegation, and governance out of the box — so you run a company, not a pile of scripts.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e❌ Runaway loops waste hundreds of dollars of tokens and max your quota before you even know what happened.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e✅ Cost tracking surfaces token budgets and throttles agents when they're out. Management prioritizes with budgets.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e❌ You have recurring jobs (customer support, social, reports) and have to remember to manually kick them off.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e✅ Heartbeats handle regular work on a schedule. Management supervises.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e❌ You have an idea, you have to find your repo, fire up Claude Code, keep a tab open, and babysit it.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e✅ Add a task in Paperclip. Your coding agent works on it until it's done. Management reviews their work.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" correctly.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003e\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAtomic execution.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTask checkout and budget enforcement are atomic, so no double-work and no runaway spend.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePersistent agent state.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAgents resume the same task context across heartbeats instead of restarting from scratch.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRuntime skill injection.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAgents can learn Paperclip workflows and project context at runtime, without retraining.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGovernance with rollback.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eApproval gates are enforced, config changes are revisioned, and bad changes can be rolled back safely.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGoal-aware execution.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTasks carry full goal ancestry so agents consistently see the \"why,\" not just a title.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePortable company templates.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eExport/import orgs, agents, and skills with secret scrubbing and collision handling.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTrue multi-company isolation.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEvery entity is company-scoped, so one deployment can run many companies with separate data and audit trails.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 a chatbot.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAgents have jobs, not chat windows.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNot an agent framework.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eWe don't tell you how to build agents. We tell you how to run a company made of them.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNot a workflow builder.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eNo drag-and-drop pipelines. Paperclip models companies — with org charts, goals, budgets, and governance.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNot a prompt manager.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAgents bring their own prompts, models, and runtimes. Paperclip manages the organization they work in.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNot a single-agent tool.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eThis is for teams. If you have one agent, you probably don't need Paperclip. If you have twenty — you definitely do.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eNot a code review tool.\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePaperclip orchestrates work, not pull requests. Bring your own review process.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eQuickstart\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-quickstart\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Quickstart\" href=\"#quickstart\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOpen source. Self-hosted. No Paperclip account required.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"npx paperclipai onboard --yes\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003enpx paperclipai onboard --yes\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThat quickstart path now defaults to trusted local loopback mode for the fastest first run. To start in authenticated/private mode instead, choose a bind preset explicitly:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"npx paperclipai onboard --yes --bind lan\n# or:\nnpx paperclipai onboard --yes --bind tailnet\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003enpx paperclipai onboard --yes --bind lan\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e or:\u003c/span\u003e\nnpx paperclipai onboard --yes --bind tailnet\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you already have Paperclip configured, rerunning \u003ccode\u003eonboard\u003c/code\u003e keeps the existing config in place. Use \u003ccode\u003epaperclipai configure\u003c/code\u003e to edit settings.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOr manually:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"git clone https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip.git\ncd paperclip\npnpm install\npnpm dev\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003egit clone https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip.git\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ecd\u003c/span\u003e paperclip\npnpm install\npnpm dev\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis starts the API server at \u003ccode\u003ehttp://localhost:3100\u003c/code\u003e. An embedded PostgreSQL database is created automatically — no setup required.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cblockquote\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eRequirements:\u003c/strong\u003e Node.js 20+, pnpm 9.15+\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/blockquote\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eFAQ\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-faq\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: FAQ\" href=\"#faq\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat does a typical setup look like?\u003c/strong\u003e\nLocally, a single Node.js process manages an embedded Postgres and local file storage. For production, point it at your own Postgres and deploy however you like. Configure projects, agents, and goals — the agents take care of the rest.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you're a solo-entreprenuer you can use Tailscale to access Paperclip on the go. Then later you can deploy to e.g. Vercel when you need it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCan I run multiple companies?\u003c/strong\u003e\nYes. A single deployment can run an unlimited number of companies with complete data isolation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHow is Paperclip different from agents like OpenClaw or Claude Code?\u003c/strong\u003e\nPaperclip \u003cem\u003euses\u003c/em\u003e those agents. It orchestrates them into a company — with org charts, budgets, goals, governance, and accountability.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhy should I use Paperclip instead of just pointing my OpenClaw to Asana or Trello?\u003c/strong\u003e\nAgent orchestration has subtleties in how you coordinate who has work checked out, how to maintain sessions, monitoring costs, establishing governance - Paperclip does this for you.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e(Bring-your-own-ticket-system is on the Roadmap)\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eDo agents run continuously?\u003c/strong\u003e\nBy default, agents run on scheduled heartbeats and event-based triggers (task assignment, @-mentions). You can also hook in continuous agents like OpenClaw. You bring your agent and Paperclip coordinates.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003ePaperclip collects anonymous usage telemetry to help us understand how the product is used and improve it. No personal information, issue content, prompts, file paths, or secrets are ever collected. Private repository references are hashed with a per-install salt before being sent.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTelemetry is \u003cstrong\u003eenabled by default\u003c/strong\u003e and can be disabled with any of the following:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eMethod\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eHow\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEnvironment variable\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ePAPERCLIP_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eStandard convention\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003eDO_NOT_TRACK=1\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCI environments\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAutomatically disabled when \u003ccode\u003eCI=true\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eConfig file\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSet \u003ccode\u003etelemetry.enabled: false\u003c/code\u003e in your Paperclip config\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" ## Summary

- Adds comprehensive release notes for `v2026.410.0`, the security
release that patches GHSA-68qg-g8mg-6pr7 (unauthenticated RCE via import
authorization bypass)
- Required before triggering the stable release workflow to publish
`2026.410.0` to npm and create the GitHub Release

## Context

The security fix (PR #3315) is already merged to master. The GHSA
advisory references `2026.410.0` as the patched version, but only canary
builds exist on npm. This PR unblocks the stable release.

## Test plan

- [x] Release notes file is valid markdown
- [ ] Merge and trigger `release.yml` workflow with `source_ref=master`,
`stable_date=2026-04-10`
- [ ] Verify `npm view paperclipai version` returns `2026.410.0`
- [ ] Verify GitHub Release `v2026.410.0` exists

enforce Model Used section in PR descriptions (#2891)

## Thinking Path

> - Paperclip orchestrates AI agents for zero-human companies
> - Agents create pull requests as part of their development workflow
> - The PR template already has a "Model Used" section (added in PR
#2552)
> - But agents were not filling it in because neither AGENTS.md nor
CONTRIBUTING.md referenced it
> - This PR updates both docs to explicitly require reading and filling
in the full PR template, including Model Used
> - The benefit is that every PR will now document which AI model
produced the change, improving traceability and auditability

## What Changed

- **CONTRIBUTING.md**: Added "Model Used (Required)" subsection under
"PR Requirements (all PRs)" and listed it in the required sections
enumeration
- **AGENTS.md**: Added new "Section 10: Pull Request Requirements"
instructing agents to read and fill in every section of the PR template
when creating PRs (including Model Used). Renumbered "Definition of
Done" to Section 11 and added PR template compliance as item 5.

## Verification

- Review `CONTRIBUTING.md` — confirm "Model Used (Required)" subsection
appears under PR Requirements
- Review `AGENTS.md` — confirm Section 10 (Pull Request Requirements)
lists all required PR template sections including Model Used
- Review `AGENTS.md` — confirm Definition of Done item 5 references PR
template compliance
- No code changes, no tests to run

## Risks

- Low risk — documentation-only changes. No code, schema, or behavioral
changes.

## Model Used

- **Provider**: Anthropic Claude
- **Model ID**: claude-opus-4-6 (1M context)
- **Capabilities**: Tool use, code execution, extended context

## Checklist

- [x] I have included a thinking path that traces from project context
to this change
- [x] I have specified the model used (with version and capability
details)
- [x] I have run tests locally and they pass
- [x] I have added or updated tests where applicable
- [x] If this change affects the UI, I have included before/after
screenshots
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation to reflect my changes
- [x] I have considered and documented any risks above
- [x] I will address all Greptile and reviewer comments before
requesting merge

title="feat(adapters): external adapter plugin system with dynamic UI parser

- Plugin loader: install/reload/remove/reinstall external adapters
  from npm packages or local directories
- Plugin store persisted at ~/.paperclip/adapter-plugins.json
- Self-healing UI parser resolution with version caching
- UI: Adapter Manager page, dynamic loader, display registry
  with humanized names for unknown adapter types
- Dev watch: exclude adapter-plugins dir from tsx watcher
  to prevent mid-request server restarts during reinstall
- All consumer fallbacks use getAdapterLabel() for consistent display
- AdapterTypeDropdown uses controlled open state for proper close behavior
- Remove hermes-local from built-in UI (externalized to plugin)
- Add docs for external adapters and UI parser contract" class="Link--secondary" href="/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/14d59da316324fa4cacf1e3393ac51d64f98115d">feat(adapters): external adapter plugin title="test(codex-local): regression for CodexRpcClient spawn ENOENT

Add a Vitest case that mocks `node:child_process.spawn` so the child
emits `error` (ENOENT) after the constructor attaches listeners.
`getQuotaWindows()` must resolve with `ok: false` instead of leaving an
unhandled `error` event on the process.

Register `packages/adapters/codex-local` in the root Vitest workspace.

Document in DEVELOPING.md that a missing `codex` binary should not take
down the API server during quota polling." class="Link--secondary" href="/paperclipai/paperclip/commit/c98af52590565835d8b5eb8dafd3ea1f907851b1">test(codex-local): regression for CodexRpcClient spawn <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Open-source orchestration for zero-human companies</h1><a id="user-content-open-source-orchestration-for-zero-human-companies" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Open-source orchestration for zero-human companies" href="#open-source-orchestration-for-zero-human-companies"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>If OpenClaw is an <em>employee</em>, Paperclip is the <em>company</em></strong></p>
<p dir="auto">Paperclip is a Node.js server and React UI that orchestrates a team of AI agents to run a business. Bring your own agents, assign goals, and track your agents' work and costs from one dashboard.</p>
<p dir="auto">It looks like a task manager — but under the hood it has org charts, budgets, governance, goal alignment, and agent coordination.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Manage business goals, not pull requests.</strong></p>

<li>✅ You have <strong>20 simultaneous Claude Code terminals</strong> open and lose track of what everyone is doing</li>
<li>✅ You want agents running <strong>autonomously 24/7</strong>, but still want to audit work and chime in when needed</li>
<li>✅ You want to <strong>monitor costs</strong> and enforce budgets</li>
<li>✅ You want a process for managing agents that <strong>feels like using a task manager</strong></li>
<li>✅ You want to manage your autonomous businesses <strong>from your phone</strong></li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Problems Paperclip solves</h2><a id="user-content-problems-paperclip-solves" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Problems Paperclip solves" href="#problems-paperclip-solves"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Without Paperclip</th>
<th>With Paperclip</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>❌ You have 20 Claude Code tabs open and can't track which one does what. On reboot you lose everything.</td>
<td>✅ Tasks are ticket-based, conversations are threaded, sessions persist across reboots.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>❌ You manually gather context from several places to remind your bot what you're actually doing.</td>
<td>✅ Context flows from the task up through the project and company goals — your agent always knows what to do and why.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>❌ Folders of agent configs are disorganized and you're re-inventing task management, communication, and coordination between agents.</td>
<td>✅ Paperclip gives you org charts, ticketing, delegation, and governance out of the box — so you run a company, not a pile of scripts.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>❌ Runaway loops waste hundreds of dollars of tokens and max your quota before you even know what happened.</td>
<td>✅ Cost tracking surfaces token budgets and throttles agents when they're out. Management prioritizes with budgets.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>❌ You have recurring jobs (customer support, social, reports) and have to remember to manually kick them off.</td>
<td>✅ Heartbeats handle regular work on a schedule. Management supervises.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>❌ You have an idea, you have to find your repo, fire up Claude Code, keep a tab open, and babysit it.</td>
<td>✅ Add a task in Paperclip. Your coding agent works on it until it's done. Management reviews their work.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<br>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Why Paperclip is special</h2><a id="user-content-why-paperclip-is-special" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Why Paperclip is special" href="#why-paperclip-is-special"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Paperclip handles the hard orchestration details correctly.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Atomic execution.</strong></td>
<td>Task checkout and budget enforcement are atomic, so no double-work and no runaway spend.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Persistent agent state.</strong></td>
<td>Agents resume the same task context across heartbeats instead of restarting from scratch.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Runtime skill injection.</strong></td>
<td>Agents can learn Paperclip workflows and project context at runtime, without retraining.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Governance with rollback.</strong></td>
<td>Approval gates are enforced, config changes are revisioned, and bad changes can be rolled back safely.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Goal-aware execution.</strong></td>
<td>Tasks carry full goal ancestry so agents consistently see the "why," not just a title.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Portable company templates.</strong></td>
<td>Export/import orgs, agents, and skills with secret scrubbing and collision handling.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>True multi-company isolation.</strong></td>
<td>Every entity is company-scoped, so one deployment can run many companies with separate data and audit trails.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<br>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Paperclip is not</h2><a id="user-content-what-paperclip-is-not" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Paperclip is not" href="#what-paperclip-is-not"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Not a chatbot.</strong></td>
<td>Agents have jobs, not chat windows.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Not an agent framework.</strong></td>
<td>We don't tell you how to build agents. We tell you how to run a company made of them.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Not a workflow builder.</strong></td>
<td>No drag-and-drop pipelines. Paperclip models companies — with org charts, goals, budgets, and governance.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Not a prompt manager.</strong></td>
<td>Agents bring their own prompts, models, and runtimes. Paperclip manages the organization they work in.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Not a single-agent tool.</strong></td>
<td>This is for teams. If you have one agent, you probably don't need Paperclip. If you have twenty — you definitely do.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Not a code review tool.</strong></td>
<td>Paperclip orchestrates work, not pull requests. Bring your own review process.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<br>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">FAQ</h2><a id="user-content-faq" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: FAQ" href="#faq"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What does a typical setup look like?</strong>
Locally, a single Node.js process manages an embedded Postgres and local file storage. For production, point it at your own Postgres and deploy however you like. Configure projects, agents, and goals — the agents take care of the rest.</p>
<p dir="auto">If you're a solo-entreprenuer you can use Tailscale to access Paperclip on the go. Then later you can deploy to e.g. Vercel when you need it.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Can I run multiple companies?</strong>
Yes. A single deployment can run an unlimited number of companies with complete data isolation.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>How is Paperclip different from agents like OpenClaw or Claude Code?</strong>
Paperclip <em>uses</em> those agents. It orchestrates them into a company — with org charts, budgets, goals, governance, and accountability.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Why should I use Paperclip instead of just pointing my OpenClaw to Asana or Trello?</strong>
Agent orchestration has subtleties in how you coordinate who has work checked out, how to maintain sessions, monitoring costs, establishing governance - Paperclip does this for you.</p>
<p dir="auto">(Bring-your-own-ticket-system is on the Roadmap)</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Do agents run continuously?</strong>
By default, agents run on scheduled heartbeats and event-based triggers (task assignment, @-mentions). You can also hook in continuous agents like OpenClaw. You bring your agent and Paperclip coordinates.</p>
<br>

<li>✅ Easy AGENTS.md configurations</li>

<li>✅ Agent Reviews and Approvals</li>
<li>⚪ Multiple Human Users</li>
<li>⚪ Cloud / Sandbox agents (e.g. Cursor / e2b agents)</li>
<li>⚪ Artifacts & Work Products</li>
<li>⚪ Memory & Knowledge</li>
<li>⚪ Enforced Outcomes</li>
<li>⚪ MAXIMIZER MODE</li>
<li>⚪ Deep Planning</li>
<li>⚪ Work Queues</li>
<li>⚪ Self-Organization</li>
<li>⚪ Automatic Organizational Learning</li>
<li>⚪ CEO Chat</li>
<li>⚪ Cloud deployments</li>
<li>⚪ Desktop App</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Telemetry</h2><a id="user-content-telemetry" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Telemetry" href="#telemetry"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Paperclip collects anonymous usage telemetry to help us understand how the product is used and improve it. No personal information, issue content, prompts, file paths, or secrets are ever collected. Private repository references are hashed with a per-install salt before being sent.</p>
<p dir="auto">Telemetry is <strong>enabled by default</strong> and can be disabled with any of the following:</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Method</th>
<th>How</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Environment variable</td>
<td><code>PAPERCLIP_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Standard convention</td>
<td><code>DO_NOT_TRACK=1</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>CI environments</td>
<td>Automatically disabled when <code>CI=true</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Config file</td>
<td>Set <code>telemetry.enabled: false</code> in your Paperclip config</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
